etymology.description: Depicts a three-legged bronze vessel -> later used for divination
etymology.interpretation: The character 鼎(dǐng, 'tripod') is a pictogram that depicts a tripod vessel with three legs, symbolizing its use in cooking and ritual sacrifices. Over time, it was also borrowed phonetically to represent the concept of divination.

examples.chinese: 商朝的青铜鼎(商朝的青銅鼎)象征着权力(象徵著權力)。
examples.pinyin: Shāng cháo de qīngtóng dǐng xiàngzhēng zhe quánlì.
examples.english: The bronze tripod from the Shang Dynasty symbolizes power.

examples.chinese: 在古代(在古代)，鼎是用来烹饪食物的器具(鼎是用來烹飪食物的器具)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài, dǐng shì yòng lái pēngrèn shíwù de qìjù.
examples.english: In ancient times, the tripod was a utensil used for cooking food.

examples.chinese: 这个博物馆里展示了一尊精美的铜鼎(這個博物館裏展示了一尊精美的銅鼎)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège bówùguǎn lǐ zhǎnshì le yī zūn jīngměi de tóngdǐng.
examples.english: This museum exhibits an exquisite bronze tripod.
